content.name: Core Supports - Assistance with Daily Life
content.description: Assistance with, and/or supervising, personal daily activities to enable the participant to live as autonomously as possible. Includes assistance with daily tasks such as showering, dressing, meal preparation, and community access.

content.name: Core Supports - Assistance with Social and Community Participation
content.description: Support to enable a participant to engage in community, social and recreational activities. Includes skill development for community participation and social interaction.

content.name: Capacity Building - Improved Daily Living Skills
content.description: Assessment, training and/or therapy to assist a participant to develop skills to live as autonomously as possible. Delivered by an allied health professional.

content.name: Capacity Building - Improved Relationships
content.description: Specialist positive behaviour support, including functional behaviour assessment, development of a behaviour support plan, and training for carers. For participants with behaviours of concern.

content.name: Capacity Building - Improved Health and Wellbeing
content.description: Dietetics, exercise physiology, and other health-related supports that build the participant's capacity. Must be directly related to disability-related health needs, not general health maintenance.
Recommendation does not reference a specific functional impairment documented in the assessment. NDIS planners require each recommended support to be tied to an evidenced impairment.
content.example_bad: It is recommended that the participant receive 5 hours per week of support worker assistance.
content.example_good: Due to the significant executive functioning deficits documented in Part D (WHODAS cognition domain score: 4/5), [Name] requires 5 hours per week of support worker assistance to maintain daily routines including medication management, meal preparation, and household tasks. Without this support, [Name] is at risk of nutritional decline and medication non-compliance.

content.description: Standardised assessment scores contradict the narrative description. A WHODAS domain score of 0-1 paired with narrative claiming severe limitations, or a score of 3-4 paired with mild language, triggers planner scrutiny.
content.example_bad: The participant scored 1 (mild) on the WHODAS mobility domain. The participant demonstrates severe and debilitating mobility impairment that prevents independent community access.
content.example_good: The participant scored 1 (mild) on the WHODAS mobility domain, indicating minimal difficulty with mobility tasks in controlled environments. However, community mobility remains impacted by fatigue and sensory overload, as documented in the functional observations.

content.description: Use of hedging or aspirational language instead of definitive clinical statements. Planners interpret vague language as uncertain clinical opinion and may reduce or deny funding.

replacement_patterns.would benefit from: requires X to maintain functional capacity in Y
replacement_patterns.may need assistance: requires daily assistance with X due to Y
replacement_patterns.could help improve: is clinically indicated to prevent deterioration of X
replacement_patterns.it is recommended: clinical assessment indicates necessity for X

content.description: Content that could apply to any participant without modification. Planners flag boilerplate text as insufficient evidence of individual assessment.
indicators: No participant name referenced in participant-specific contexts
No reference to specific observed behaviours or assessment dates
Generic functional descriptions without individual examples

content.description: Recommendations fail to demonstrate the 'reasonable and necessary' criteria. Each support must show: (1) related to the disability, (2) not duplicating other services, (3) effective and beneficial, (4) represents value for money, (5) likely to be needed for the funding period.
required_elements: Direct link to disability-related functional impairment
Evidence that mainstream services cannot meet the need
Demonstration of support effectiveness or expected outcomes
Consideration of informal and natural supports

content.template: Based on the functional capacity assessment, [Name] requires [X] hours per week of support worker assistance for [specific tasks]. This support is necessary due to [specific impairment documented in Part X], which results in [functional impact]. Without this level of support, [consequence]. Current informal supports from [support network] provide [Y] hours per week, which is insufficient to meet [Name]'s needs because [reason]. The recommended [X] hours represents the minimum required to maintain [Name]'s safety and functional capacity.

Clinical assessment indicates that [Name] requires [frequency] sessions of [therapy type] to address [specific functional goal]. Standardised assessment using [tool name] yielded a score of [score], indicating [severity level] impairment in [domain]. Without ongoing therapeutic intervention, [Name] is at risk of [deterioration description]. This support aligns with NDIS Capacity Building category [number] and is delivered by a registered [provider type].

[Name] requires [X] hours per week of community access support to achieve their stated goal of [participant goal]. Due to [specific impairments], [Name] is unable to independently [specific activities] without support. The support worker will provide [types of assistance], enabling [Name] to develop skills in [skill areas] while maintaining community connections essential for [wellbeing outcome].

Following assessment, [Name] requires [specific AT item] to address [functional limitation]. Current strategies of [current_approach] are inadequate because [reason]. The recommended assistive technology will enable [Name] to [functional outcome], reducing reliance on [current support] and promoting independence in [activity domain]. This item falls within NDIS AT category [category] and represents value for money compared to [alternative].

[Name] presents with behaviours of concern including [specific behaviours] which impact [functional domains]. Functional behaviour assessment indicates these behaviours are maintained by [function]. Without specialist positive behaviour support, [Name] is at risk of [risk outcomes]. A comprehensive behaviour support plan, including [components], will address the underlying function of these behaviours while building [Name]'s capacity in [alternative skills].

0.label: None
0.ndis_implication: Does not support funding for this domain unless functional observations contradict

1.label: Mild
1.ndis_implication: Weak justification for dedicated supports; must be paired with strong functional observation evidence

2.label: Moderate
2.ndis_implication: Supports justifiable when paired with functional impact evidence and participant goals

3.label: Severe
3.ndis_implication: Strong justification for ongoing supports; corresponds to significant functional limitation

4.label: Extreme/Cannot do
4.ndis_implication: Strongest justification; indicates complete dependence on supports in this domain

content.scoring_note: Overall disability score is calculated as the mean of domain scores, converted to a 0-100 scale. Scores above 50 indicate significant overall disability.

much_less_than_most.ndis_implication: Significant sensory processing difference; strong justification for environmental modifications and sensory-based interventions

less_than_most.ndis_implication: Notable sensory processing difference; supports justifiable for targeted sensory strategies

just_like_most.ndis_implication: Typical range; does not independently support sensory-based recommendations

more_than_most.ndis_implication: Notable sensory processing difference; supports justifiable for targeted sensory strategies

much_more_than_most.ndis_implication: Significant sensory processing difference; strong justification for comprehensive sensory supports

content.scoring_note: Clinically significant patterns require scores in 'Much Less Than Most' or 'Much More Than Most' ranges. Patterns across multiple quadrants indicate pervasive sensory processing difficulties.

10-19.label: Likely to be well
10-19.ndis_implication: Does not independently support mental health-related functional supports

20-24.label: Likely to have a mild mental disorder
20-24.ndis_implication: Moderate justification when paired with functional impact evidence

25-29.label: Likely to have a moderate mental disorder

30-50.label: Likely to have a severe mental disorder
30-50.ndis_implication: Strong justification for comprehensive psychosocial supports; indicates significant functional impact

content.scoring_note: The K10 measures non-specific psychological distress over the past 30 days. Scores should be interpreted alongside functional observations and clinical interview data.

preferred_phrases: significantly impacts [Name]'s capacity to independently manage...
without support, [Name] would be unable to...
this highlights the ongoing need for...
reduced functional capacity rather than lack of skill
demonstrates a persistent pattern of functional limitation in...
[Name]'s disability directly results in an inability to...
the cumulative impact of these impairments means that...
content.usage_guidance: Always tie functional impact language to specific observed behaviours and assessment evidence. Advocacy language should be factual and evidence-based, not emotionally charged.

source: NDIS Best Practice Clinical Writing Guide

preferred_phrases: this support is reasonable and necessary because...
without this support, [Name] is at demonstrable risk of...
mainstream services are unable to meet this need because...
this represents value for money compared to...
the evidence supports that this level of support is the minimum required to...
this support directly addresses the functional impact of [Name]'s disability on...
content.usage_guidance: Every recommendation must explicitly address the reasonable and necessary criteria. Frame recommendations as clinical necessities supported by evidence, not aspirational goals.

source: NDIS Best Practice Clinical Writing Guide
